title: Monday.com Form, Capturing Signature and putting in PDF
excerpt: We have created a form, and on that form is a signature where the tech signs it stating that he has completed a certain task. We used the signature question on Monday.com and everything works great - except that the PDF …

posts.author: tony_latino (Tony Latino)
posts.cookedHtml: We have created a form, and on that form is a signature where the tech signs it stating that he has completed a certain task. We used the signature question on Monday.com and everything works great - except that the PDF that we need to show the client displays “image.png” instead of the signature itself. Is there a way to display the signature on the PDF?

posts.author: tiffany.buchanan (Tiffany_b)
posts.cookedHtml: I would love to know how you are inserting a field for the tech to sign. I’m right there in creating a form right now!

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Tony!

Welcome to the Community - thanks for your question

So that we can ensure we are on the same page, are you able to clarify which column you’re using for the tech to input his signature? Additionally, are you able to let us know how you’re downloading the form?

We look forward to hearing from you.

Best,
Bianca

posts.author: lindsayjeans (lindsay)
posts.cookedHtml:
@BiancaT I am having the exact same problem, if you wouldn’t mind answering for me as well…

In the “Form view”, when you click “add a new question” there is an option to add a “signature”.

You can then click on “Form Response Viewer”, when you click on that view, there is a button that says “Download as PDF”. When you open the PDF, the signatures don’t show up on the PDF, it just shows up as “signature.png”.

Is there a way to make those png files show as the signatures?

Thanks so much!

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @lindsayjeans,

Firstly, welcome to the community!

In regard to the roadblock you’re experiencing, I too have been able to reproduce this behavior when downloading forms that include signatures. Please allow me to follow up internally, and update you once we hear back from our specialist team.

Thank you in advance for your patience!

Best,
Bianca

posts.author: lindsayjeans (lindsay)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thank You Bianca, I would really appreciate your help on this!

Lindsay

posts.author: kconlon (Kimmie Conlon)
posts.cookedHtml: When will this issue be resolved?

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @lindsayjeans and @kconlon,

Apologies for the delayed update! Whilst I am still waiting on confirmation from our developers, it does appear that at this time it is expected that signature will not display on the PDF. I certainly recognise that this is clearly not ideal, and will ensure this feedback is discussed with our product team. I am sorry for the setback!

Once I hear more from the developers, I will be sure to update this thread.

Best,
Bianca

posts.author: HBG (Victor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey,

It seems as this issue bother many users and I wanna add myself to this list.

I don’t understand the point of capturing signatures on a form if it cannot be displayed. in what way having a signature file without the content/terms of which the signature was captured with serve anyone?

DO you anticipate that this will be resolved anytime soon?

Thanks in advance!

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: Internal - waiting on update from ask-workforms channel: Slack

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @HBG, @lindsayjeans and @kconlon,

Thanks for your patience with us while we waited to hear from our developers.

That said, the team have confirmed that they’re aware of the setback, and that there are plans to refactor the signature into the workforms result, so that it pulls when exporting to PDF, however at this time, they cannot confirm a specific timeline for this feature release. I apologise for the lack of clarity here, however please be assured that the team hope to support this functionality in the near future.

Thank you in advance for your understanding!

Best,
Bianca

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey everyone, thanks for your patience with me! I have heard back from the dev team who confirmed that as of now this particular feature improvement is not immediately planned, due to other priorities and team capacity - however it is being considered in our next iteration. I understand this isn’t the concrete confirmation you may have been looking for however we will be sure to keep you in the loop as we’re made known to new developments. We appreciate your understanding in advance!

posts.cookedHtml: Something related that the development team should consider when printing a signature image to a PDF, is to include the user name and date/time of the applied signature directly below the image. This would help Monday.com support e-signature requirements regarding validity of signature images.

posts.author: triplermarketing (Matt)
posts.cookedHtml:
DocuGen has been a solid option to not only capture the signature in the PDF, but to generate the PDF in a file column.

You will have to set up a template, but it is fairly straight forward. They do not have the automation to automatically create the PDF when an item is created, so we set up a work around. We used the DocuGen automation, when status changes to “x” create the PDF, and then add a Monday automation to set the status to “x” when an item is created.

DocuGen

DocuGen

Generate documents from your monday.com boards

Hope this help!

posts.author: johannesgeyer (Johannes Geyer)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone!

There’s a way to capture the signature with a monday form and automatically create a PDF document by using the DocExport app.
You can even use a monday automation with the trigger ‘When item created’ to generate the file automatically after form submission and save it to a file column.

The placeholder needed in the template is {{Image(100;0):Item.Signature}}

Here’s a quick video tutorial of how to set it up. It’s for the use case ‘Event Ticket’ but it’s the same logic for many other use cases like contracts, audit reports and so on:

cc @SarahHalo @AleK @Maloner @Mondae

posts.author: exoticvetclinic (Cori)

posts.author: JenefaJ (Jenefa)
posts.cookedHtml:
That’s a common limitation with monday’s native signature question — when you export or generate a PDF, it often just shows the placeholder file name (like image.png) instead of the actual signature image.

With The PDF Maker inside monday, you can fix this:

  • Signature Rendering → When you add a signature field to a form, The PDF Maker pulls the actual signature image into your PDF instead of just the file name.

  • Custom PDF Layouts → You can design the PDF template so the signature appears exactly where you want (bottom of the form, in a sign-off box, etc.).

  • Compliance-Ready → Each signature is stored with signer details, date, and an audit trail to make it legally valid.

  • Automation → The signed PDF can be automatically attached back to the monday item so it’s ready to share with your client.

So yes — the workaround is to use The PDF Maker’s PDF generation rather than monday’s default export. That way your clients see the actual signature in the final document, not “image.png.”

Guide: How to collect eSignatures on your documents using monday.com

posts.author: zoe.sgt (Zoe | SGTLab)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey everyone

Has anyone here found a satisfying solution for generating PDFs with signatures?
We ran into the same issue and ended up building our own tool to automate it, like in this video.

We built this because we needed it ourselves and haven’t found a satisfying solution out there yet

It’s open for some early users while we’re gathering feedback. If you’d like to test it, you can try it out here.

Always curious to hear how others are solving this too!
